chunks.title: v2.2.5
chunks.raw_text: Repository: netdata/netdata Release: v2.2.5 Tag: v2.2.5; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-02-12T15:27:24Z; created_at: 2025-02-12T14:02:53Z Release notes: Netdata v2.2.5 is a patch release to address issues discovered since [v2.2.4](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/releases/tag/v2.2.4). This patch release provides the following bug fixes and updates: - Fixed MariaDB collector to handle CPU time values reported in microseconds since version 11.4.5 ([#19618](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19618), [@ilyam8](https://github.com/ilyam8)) - Changed clickhouse_replicated_readonly_tables info to clarify read-only state conditions for replicated tables ([#19616](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19616), [@ilyam8](https://github.com/ilyam8)) - Fixed indirect cloud access to function properly when an Agent is claimed but temporarily offline by using Parent's claim ID ([#19611](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19611), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou)) - Added data interpolation to median and percentile calculations in the stats.d plugin ([#19606](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19606), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou)) - Fixed potential database access issues during shutdown by properly closing the database when startup errors occur ([#19600](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19600), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag)) - Fixed a memory corruption issue by properly dequeuing contexts from hub before deletion ([#19599](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19599), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou)) - Fixed various issues in journal watcher, including improved event parsing, corrected path handling, and resolved memory-related bugs ([#19592](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19592), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou)) - Fixed NVMe device discovery to properly handle device paths in nvme-cli v2.11 ([#19577](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19577), [@ilyam8](https://github.com/ilyam8)) - Fixed MySQL collector to properly respect the global variables refresh interval instead of collecting them on every iteration ([#19549](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19549), [@arkamar](https://github.com/arkamar)) - Fixed several CI/build system issues, including static build conditions, POWER8+ build hosts, Ubuntu runner versions, and compiler flags ([#19575](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19575), [#19579](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19579), [#19614](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19614), [#19619](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19619), [@Ferroin](https://github.com/Ferroin)) - Fixed Amazon Linux aarch64 package builds by migrating to GitHub's ARM CI runners ([#19427](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19427), [@Ferroin](https://github.com/Ferroin)) ## Support options As we grow, we stay committed to providing the best support ever seen from an open-source solution. Should you encounter an issue with any of the changes made in this release or any feature in the Netdata Agent, feel free to contact us through one of the following channels: - [Netdata Learn](https://learn.netdata.cloud): Find documentation, guides, and reference material for monitoring and troubleshooting your systems with Netdata. - [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/issues): Make use of the Netdata repository to report bugs or open a new feature request. - [GitHub Discussions](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/discussions): Join the conversation around the Netdata development process and be a part of it. - [Community Forums](https://community.netdata.cloud/): Visit the Community Forums and contribute to the collaborative knowledge base. - [Discord Server](https://discord.gg/2eduZdSeC7): Jump into the Netdata Discord and hang out with like-minded sysadmins, DevOps, SREs, and other troubleshooters. More than 2000 engineers are already using it!
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the netdata/netdata release v2.2.5, the patch addresses issues from v2.2.4 with fixes including MariaDB collector handling of CPU time in microseconds since version 11.4.5, improved clickhouse_replicated_readonly_tables info, indirect cloud access using Parent's claim ID when Agent is offline, data interpolation for median and percentile in stats.d, proper database closure on startup errors, memory corruption fix by dequeuing contexts, journal watcher fixes, NVMe device discovery for nvme-cli v2.11, MySQL collector respecting global variables refresh interval, and various CI/build system fixes including Amazon Linux aarch64 package builds migration to GitHub ARM runners.

chunks.title: v2.3.0
chunks.raw_text: Repository: netdata/netdata Release: v2.3.0 Tag: v2.3.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-03-19T16:26:55Z; created_at: 2025-03-19T14:53:39Z Release notes: ## Table of Contents - [Netdata Growth](#v230-netdata-open-source-growth) - [Summary](#v230-release-summary) - [Highlights](#v230-release-highlights) - [Acknowledgments](#v230-acknowledgments) - [Contributions](#v230-contributions) - [Collectors](#v230-contributions-collectors) - [Packaging/Installation](#v230-contributions-packaging) - [Documentation](#v230-contributions-documentation) - [Other Notable Changes](#v230-contributions-other) - [Deprecation notice](#v230-deprecation-notice) - [Support options](#v230-support-options) ### Netdata Growth - **1.5 million downloads per day** - **73.7k GitHub stars!** - **656.1M Docker Hub pulls!** Netdata continues to experience phenomenal growth, with over **1.5 million downloads daily** through Cloudflare and Docker Hub, fueling user observability worldwide. Thanks to your unwavering support ❤️, Netdata is the leader in the observability category in the CNCF landscape, ahead of all other solutions, including Elasticsearch, Grafana, and Prometheus, in GitHub stars. This demonstrates the trust and admiration of our community. This success drives rapid adoption among enterprises, reflecting the growing recognition of Netdata as the **go-to observability solution** for both **cloud-native** and **on-premises environments**. Our commitment remains steadfast: to deliver cutting-edge, **AI-powered observability** with unmatched performance and simplicity—all while being significantly more affordable. We are also proud to see our users and customers experience high-scale setups, achieving reliable multi-million samples/s setups, effortlessly, streamlining their operations with Netdata. As we evolve, our focus on empowering businesses with **higher-fidelity AI insights** ensures Netdata remains the easiest and fastest way to optimize infrastructure and applications at any scale. 🚀 Do you like Netdata? [Give Netdata a ⭐ too, on GitHub!](https://github.com/netdata/netdata) ## Release Summary Netdata 2.3 delivers significant enhancements to monitoring reliability and scalability: - **Crash Handling & Reporting**: A zero-sampling system that captures and analyzes agent crashes with complete diagnostic information, significantly improving reliability across diverse environments. - **Extreme Cardinality Protection**: Automatic safeguards that maintain performance in high-scale environments with millions of time series while intelligently managing metadata retention. - **Nodes Ephemerality & Streaming Alerts**: A sophisticated approach to handling node connections in distributed environments, reducing alert noise by distinguishing between permanent and ephemeral nodes. - **SNMP Service Discovery**: A new system automatically finds and monitors SNMP-enabled devices on configured networks, eliminating manual configuration. ## Release Highlights ### Nodes Ephemerality & Streaming Alerts Netdata 2.3 implements a more sophisticated approach to handling node connections in distributed environments. We now define ephemeral nodes as "nodes that are expected to disconnect without raising alerts", enabling smarter monitoring of dynamic infrastructure. | Feature | Description | |------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------| | **Smart Node Classification** | Distinguish between permanent infrastructure (servers) and ephemeral resources (containers, auto-scaling instances) | | **Targeted Alerting**...
chunks.memory_unit_text: In the netdata/netdata release v2.3.0, the major release delivers crash handling and reporting with zero-sampling, extreme cardinality protection, nodes ephemerality and streaming alerts to distinguish permanent and ephemeral nodes, and SNMP service discovery for automatic detection and monitoring of SNMP-enabled devices.

Repository: netdata/netdata
Release: v2.3.1
Tag: v2.3.1; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-03-24T21:25:47Z; created_at: 2025-03-24T16:33:59Z
Release notes:
Netdata v2.3.1 is a patch release to address issues discovered since [v2.3.0](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/releases/tag/v2.3.0).
This patch release provides the following bug fixes and updates:
- Fixed debug information handling by including it in default builds while disabling separate debuginfo packages for Debian-based distributions ([#19946](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19946), [#19948](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19948) [@Ferroin](https://github.com/Ferroin))
- Fixed static build configuration to avoid unnecessary libunwind compilation ([#19939](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19939), [@Ferroin](https://github.com/Ferroin))
- Improved detection of low memory conditions with more aggressive monitoring ([#19938](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19938), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed installation path for updater script crontab configuration ([#19935](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19935), [@ralphm](https://github.com/ralphm))
- Fixed validation of database page size limits for 32-bit compression format ([#19932](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19932), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Fixed compilation issues when building without database engine support or with address sanitizer enabled ([#19930](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19930), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Added additional system resource metrics to status file including memory usage and enhanced out-of-memory protection information ([#19928](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19928), [#19937](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19937) [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed security issue by preventing exposure of absolute file paths in web server responses ([#19925](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19925), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed security vulnerability in daemon status file handling by using file descriptor-based permissions to prevent race conditions ([#19924](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19924), [@Ferroin](https://github.com/Ferroin))
- Removed insecure SVG generation endpoint to prevent potential code injection vulnerabilities ([#19919](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19919), [@ilyam8](https://github.com/ilyam8))
- Fixed unaligned memory access in socket message buffer by properly aligning memory structures ([#19917](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19917), [@vkalintiris](https://github.com/vkalintiris))
- Fixed ACLK synchronization by ensuring thread initialization completes before proceeding with startup ([#19916](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19916), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Fixed issue where commands could be queued before ACLK initialization was complete ([#19914](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19914), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed potential crash when database engine encounters null data files during range operations ([#19913](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19913), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed Agent status reporting to handle first-run scenarios when no previous status file exists ([#19912](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19912), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Added initial implementation of libbacktrace for improved crash diagnostics ([#19910](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19910), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed reliability calculation to properly handle normal Agent exit cases ([#19909](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19909), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Added enhanced shutdown diagnostics with timeouts and improved system information in crash reports including cloud provider details ([#19903](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19903), [@ktsaou](https://github....

Release notes:
Netdata v2.3.2 is a patch release to address issues discovered since [v2.3.1](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/releases/tag/v2.3.1).
This patch release provides the following bug fixes and updates:
- Fixed journal file creation reliability with improved error handling and simplified allocation process ([#20018](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20018), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed leakage of build environment identifiers by blacklisting GitHub runner machine IDs ([#20016](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20016), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed potential memory access violations by adding validation for journal file headers and page boundaries ([#20013](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20013), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Fixed a rare crash condition by properly reinitializing data collection for obsolete or archived dimensions ([#20007](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20007), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed MegaCLI collector to properly handle missing battery backup units ([#20008](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20008), [@ilyam8](https://github.com/ilyam8))
- Changed UUID generation to use version 4 format for better uniqueness ([#20002](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20002), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Added detection for additional CI environment variables to automatically disable telemetry ([#19999](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19999), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed Agent status system to handle null UUIDs and improved tracking of shutdown time, crash counts, and connection states ([#19996](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19996), [#20003](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20003), [#20011](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/20011) [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Added detailed worker thread status information and enhanced crash diagnostics capabilities ([#19992](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19992), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed potential crash in Windows perflib collector when handling null pointers ([#19985](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19985), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed error reporting to preserve errno values during out-of-memory conditions ([#19984](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19984), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed potential crashes when handling empty data arrays ([#19983](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19983), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed Agent shutdown by properly joining ACLK and metadata threads before closing database connections ([#19980](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19980), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Fixed random crashes during shutdown by avoiding precompiled database statements for host metadata ([#19978](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19978), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Limited maximum database file size to 1GB to optimize memory usage during file operations ([#19977](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19977), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Fixed crash in variable lookup function when processing search results with scores ([#19972](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19972), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed ACLK synchronization thread shutdown with better termination sequence and timeout handling for stuck operations ([#19966](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19966), [@stelfrag](https://github.com/stelfrag))
- Improved Parent node startup performance by preloading UUIDs into metrics registry for faster initialization ([#19964](https://github.com/netdata/netdata/pull/19964), [@ktsaou](https://github.com/ktsaou))
- Fixed Windows installer to properly manage configurat...

Repository: netdata/netdata Release: v2.5.0 Tag: v2.5.0; draft: False; prerelease: False; published_at: 2025-05-05T22:41:52Z; created_at: 2025-05-05T15:57:50Z Release notes: ## Table of Contents - [Summary](#v250-release-summary) - [Acknowledgments](#v250-acknowledgments) - [Contributions](#v250-contributions) - [Collectors](#v250-contributions-collectors) - [Packaging/Installation](#v250-contributions-packaging) - [Documentation](#v250-contributions-documentation) - [Other Notable Changes](#v250-contributions-other) - [Deprecation notice](#v250-deprecation-notice) - [Support options](#v250-support-options) ## Release Summary Netdata v2.5.0 continues our commitment to stability with significant improvements to system robustness. This release focuses on eliminating potential crashes, resolving memory issues, and enhancing thread management across the codebase. chunks.memory_unit_text: In the netdata/netdata release v2.10.3, the patch addresses issues from v2.10.2 with fixes including a per-PID shared-memory pool leak in ebpf.plugin that caused CPU pegging, switching SNMP collector's primary uptime source to SNMP-FRAMEWORK-MIB::snmpEngineTime to avoid TimeTicks wrap, splitting dynamic configuration job-name validation per domain to allow dots in service discovery, vnode, and secret-store names, and removing unused extra_details field from go.d/powerstore Hardware struct to fix decoding errors.

questions.query_text: In the netdata/netdata releases, which release introduces a generic SQL collector (alpha) for monitoring any SQL database with custom queries?

answer_support.answer_text: generic SQL collector (alpha) for monitoring any SQL database with custom queries

questions.query_text: Across the netdata/netdata releases, how does the SNMP-related change described in v2.3.0 differ from the SNMP-related change described in v2.6.0?

options.text: v2.3.0 introduces SNMP service discovery for automatic detection and monitoring of SNMP-enabled devices, while v2.6.0 introduces SNMP profile-based collection with 100+ device profiles (alpha) and auto-detection for specific vendors like Cisco, Palo Alto, and F5.
v2.3.0 removes SNMP support entirely, while v2.6.0 adds it back.
Both releases only fix SNMP collector bugs without introducing new features.
v2.3.0 adds SNMP profile-based collection, while v2.6.0 adds SNMP service discovery.

questions.expected_answer: v2.3.0 introduces SNMP service discovery for automatic detection and monitoring of SNMP-enabled devices, while v2.6.0 introduces SNMP profile-based collection with 100+ device profiles (alpha) and auto-detection for specific vendors like Cisco, Palo Alto, and F5.
